profileSummary: Where I've worked and what I've focused on across product design, frontend development, and teaching.

bullets: Apply UX thinking in live educational environments shaped by clarity, accessibility, and structure.
Design learning experiences for a wide range of students, including SEND learners.
Reduce cognitive load through sequencing, scaffolding, and clearer information architecture.
Use performance data and behavioural feedback to refine how information is understood.
Communicate complex ideas clearly to students, parents, and senior leaders.

timeline.company: Winterhill School, Placement A / Ecclesfield School, Placement B
bullets: Designed and delivered curriculum-aligned lessons across placement schools.
Iterated quickly based on observation, coaching, and classroom feedback.
Developed a more disciplined clarity-first approach to instructional and experience design.
Defined and implemented a platform-wide UX vision aligned to product strategy.
Led continuous discovery through client interviews, usability testing, and behavioural analytics.
Used Pendo, FullStory, and Google Analytics to find friction and tighten key journeys.
Reduced support tickets and bug reports through targeted UX improvements.
Improved feature adoption and engagement through iterative, evidence-led changes.
Led the move from a legacy UI toward a scalable component-based design system.
Mentored two graduate developers and pushed user-centred thinking deeper into the team.
Contributed directly to roadmap conversations alongside the Head of Product.
Designed and built SaaS features across web and tablet products.
Worked closely with product and engineering in agile delivery, moving between UX decisions and implementation.
Shipped UX improvements as working product, not just Figma files.
Moved deeper into hands-on frontend delivery, translating interface decisions into shipped web experiences.
Strengthened the development side of my practice in HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and React.
Built technical realism, tighter collaboration, and fewer handoff gaps into my design approach.
Worked across UX, UI, and development, helping close the gap between concept work and implementation.
Focused on making digital experiences clearer, more trustworthy, and easier to navigate.
Moved beyond visual design into broader product thinking and delivery.
Started shaping interfaces, flows, and visual systems for digital products early in my career.
Turned a graphic design background into a more strategic UX practice.
Built the visual discipline I still bring into product work.

education: BA Graphic Design, User Experience, First Class Honours, Sheffield Hallam University
